skill.name: intercom-automation
skill.description: Automate Intercom conversations, contacts, companies, segments, admins, and support workflow actions through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Intercom automation using Rube MCP tool calls.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found false positives from Markdown inline code, tables, and prose. The real risk is networked Intercom automation through Rube MCP.

medium_findings.title: Networked Intercom Data Modification Through MCP
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill connects Rube MCP and authorizes Intercom OAuth. It guides replies, assignments, closures, reopen actions, contact detachments, and customer data listing. These actions can affect live support records without strong approval controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network dependency and Intercom-changing workflows are explicit. The function is legitimate, but business-impact risk is clear.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The external command hits are Markdown inline references to Rube and Intercom tools. They name parameters and example values, not shell commands or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is in Markdown documentation. It names MCP tools and parameters, with no execution primitive.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Hit Is Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak crypto hits cite the skill description and an admin lookup step. No hashing algorithm, encryption operation, credential handling, or crypto API appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain Intercom automation prose and admin selection guidance. They do not contain cryptographic code or algorithm names.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Hits Are Intercom Resource Lookups
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The reconnaissance hits are Intercom lookup operations for conversations, contacts, admins, and segments. They do not inspect the host system or enumerate local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations describe Intercom API fields and tool rows. They do not describe operating system reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Service Connection
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to add an external Rube MCP server and complete Intercom OAuth. Workspace owners should review this trusted network integration before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth workflow are explicit. The risk depends on user authorization and workspace policy.

dangerous_patterns.title: State-Changing Intercom Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents actions that create, reply to, assign, close, reopen, and detach Intercom records. These actions can change production customer support state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The listed tool names are direct Intercom mutation actions. The documentation also warns about duplicate sends and idempotency.

content.value_statement: Support teams lose time switching between Intercom tasks and tool schemas. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Intercom OAuth connection checks.
Lists, searches, retrieves, and creates Intercom conversations.
Supports replies, assignments, closing, and reopening conversations.
Searches contacts and retrieves contact tags, segments, and external IDs.
Lists admins, teams, segments, counts, companies, and company segments.
Documents pagination, structured filters, admin ID resolution, and retry pitfalls.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Intercom connection before use.
Does not include executable scripts or direct API client code.
Depends on current Rube tool schemas, so users must search tools first.
State-changing actions can affect live Intercom records if permissions are broad.

use_cases.title: Triage Support Conversations
use_cases.description: Find open conversations, inspect their current state, assign them to the right admin, and close resolved threads.

use_cases.title: Review Customer Context
use_cases.description: Search contacts, inspect tags and segments, and connect customer records to current conversations before responding.

use_cases.title: Audit Workspace Metadata
use_cases.description: List admins, teams, segments, counts, and companies to prepare reporting or cleanup tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Check Intercom Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the intercom automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and that the Intercom connection is active. Search tools first and report the connection status.

prompt_templates.title: Find Customer Conversations
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Intercom conversations for this customer context. Use current Rube tool schemas, explain the filters used, and show the matching conversation summaries.

prompt_templates.title: Reply and Assign Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the conversation state, resolve the correct admin ID, draft a reply, and assign the conversation. Ask before sending any visible customer message.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Segments and Companies
prompt_templates.prompt: Review contact, company, segment, and count data for this account. Handle pagination, note approximate counts, and summarize cleanup recommendations.

output_examples.input: Verify whether Intercom is connected through Rube MCP.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches available Rube tools, checks Intercom connection status, and reports whether OAuth setup is complete.

output_examples.input: Find open conversations for a user email and assign the newest one.
output_examples.output: The assistant searches contacts, finds related conversations, resolves the admin ID, and asks for confirmation before assigning the conversation.

output_examples.input: Summarize the segments attached to a company and its main contact.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves company and contact segment data, explains any pagination, and summarizes the segment membership in plain language.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so requests match the current schema.
Confirm admin IDs and customer-visible message bodies before state-changing actions.
Track sent replies and retries to avoid duplicate Intercom messages.
anti_patterns: Do not send replies or close conversations without reviewing the current conversation state.
Do not assume free-text search works when structured filters are required.
Do not use broad OAuth permissions in production without workspace approval.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Intercom?
faq.answer: It uses Rube MCP and Composio Intercom tools. Users must configure Rube MCP and complete Intercom OAuth.

faq.question: Can it send customer-visible replies?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents reply workflows, so users should confirm message content before sending.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown workflow guide for using MCP tools.

faq.question: Why must tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas can change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameters.

faq.question: What Intercom objects are covered?
faq.answer: It covers conversations, contacts, companies, segments, counts, admins, and teams.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The main risk is authorized network access that can change live Intercom support data.
